title: The End of Episode 10
wikitext:
[]: The End of Episode 10             Quote Post by Tumbling Down » Sun Aug 03, 2014 11:12 am This is the newest and dumbest in my long line of dumb threads. You have been warned. Do you think Misato and/or Asuka were purposely trying to arouse Shinj? It's definitely in-character for Misato, but not for Asuka. The situation is definitely funnier (to me, at least) if they weren't doing it intentionally.

[]:             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Sun Aug 03, 2014 12:13 pm ^ When you say arouse, do you mean to cause him to get a boner or to get his interest?

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sun Aug 03, 2014 12:23 pm ...weird question, really. Misato was just making fun of his generall awkwardness like she's been doing all the time, see the dinner scene with ritsuko from ep 5 for other prime examples. Asuka was basically mildly annoyed about the shampoo-projectile and subsequent tickle attack. Although you could, in some broad sense, see the use of some "Let me see your boo-boo" rethoric, however jokingly used, as Misato trying to play a maternal role (their following discussion about the... things in Asuka's file showing this on a more serious level), but only much later do we learn that despite the way they superficially seem to get along, Asuka actually secretly hates Misato and hasn't shown her her true self at all- It helps that Misato herself wants very hard to believe that she's all unaffected/cheerful now after her own nasty youth trauma/post-impact-catatonia, and when she's operating from this position, she's going to believe that it's possible for others - hence the words she picks as a comfort, "Don't worry, that was a long time ago..." They both wouldn't like any old stuff to be brought up in a way that would call their capabilities in doubt, since they, in different ways,want to conjure up an image of independence and capability. That's something they have in common, for better or for worse, and Misato generally has a policy of at least trying to talk to the children like/ respect them as people who can make choices, although we see this most often with Shinji. (" No more excuses! Do what you really want to do.") Respect? Irresponsible ambivalence? It's not really an obvious fingerpointing exercise as Misato herself is really trying to find a line (with both of them) and frequently wonders if she handlded particular situations right. It's not a lack of interest or good will, but there are many external factors at work (the whole NERV situation), and well, the fact that you usually get to start out with a baby and have a learning curve. Of course, it is also an example of how your own personal denial can distort your perception of the outside world at large, in the sense that this might or might not have contributed to keeping Asuka from those much-needed hugs.

[]:             Quote Post by Jäeger » Mon Aug 04, 2014 11:10 am Shinji doesn't need such crappy excuses to masturbate

[]:             Quote Post by Dataprime » Mon Aug 04, 2014 12:15 pm Misato was just teasing him. Like she always does.

[]: Re: The End of Episode 10             Quote Post by BC Baron » Tue Aug 05, 2014 12:45 am Tumbling Down wrote: It's definitely in-character for Misato, but not for Asuka.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Tue Aug 05, 2014 2:31 am I don't think that was more of some mischievous teasing, or maybe they were genuine and forgot that Shinji was on the other side of the barrier, in fact IMO not knowing if Asuka and Misato did it intentionally or not make the scene funnier.

[]:             Quote Post by Aether » Mon Nov 10, 2014 6:02 pm I can't help but wonder, either what kind of theoretical plane did Shinji throw that on, or what strange FARian sex position she was attempting that made that little interaction, well, possible? If you consider the fact that the bottle probably floats, and that thrown objects move in a parabola, and that she was probably partly underwater, just. . . how?
summary:
[]: The End of Episode 10             Quote Post by Tumbling Down » Sun Aug 03, 2014 11:12 am This is the newest and dumbest in my long line of dumb threads. You have been warned. Do you think Misato and/or Asuka were purposely trying to arouse Shinj? It's definitely in-character for Misato, but not for Asuka. The situation is definitely funnier (to me, at least) if they weren't doing it intentionally.
